CompensationBand.description:
Compensation band.

Callers are expected to ensure `min <= max`. JSON Schema cannot express
cross-field numeric inequalities without `allOf`/`oneOf` gymnastics; the
invariant is documented here and enforced by `corpospec-validate`.

equity_percentage.description:
Equity component of total compensation. Prose because real records
carry single percentages (`"60%"`), ranges (`"0.1-0.3%"`), and
annotated percentages (`"100% (indirect via Beevelop UG)"`). A
stricter numeric type would truncate or reject every real record.

max.description: Upper bound of the band, in currency units. Must be `>= min`.

min.description: Lower bound of the band, in currency units.

note.description: Prose annotation (midpoint guidance, market context, etc.).

CompensationSchedule.description:
Compensation schedule for a role.

Encodes a two-tier plan: an initial (`pre_pre_seed_*`) rate, followed by
a higher (`post_pre_seed_*`) rate that activates on a named trigger.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency of every monetary field below.

post_pre_seed_eur.description: Annual gross compensation in the post-trigger period (currency units).

post_pre_seed_trigger.description: Event that activates the post-trigger tier (see `CompensationTrigger`).

pre_pre_seed_eur.description: Annual gross compensation in the pre-trigger period (currency units).

pre_pre_seed_start_date.description: ISO date on which the pre-trigger compensation tier starts to accrue.

CompensationTrigger.description:
Event that triggers the post-pre-seed compensation tier to activate.

Stored as a machine-readable discriminator so downstream tooling can
reason about compensation scenarios without parsing prose.

oneOf.description: Closing of a pre-seed funding round.
Closing of a seed funding round.
Achievement of a named revenue threshold.
Legal formation of the employing entity.
Any other trigger documented in prose on the role record.

EmployerContributions.description:
Employer social-insurance contribution breakdown (German system).

All percentages are expressed as a fraction of gross salary, 0.0–100.0.
These are employer-side contributions; employee-side is mirrored via
deductions on the payslip.

arbeitslosenversicherung_pct.description:
Employer share of unemployment insurance (Arbeitslosenversicherung),
percent of gross salary. Scope: Germany (§ 346 SGB III).

krankenversicherung_pct.description:
Employer share of statutory health insurance (Krankenversicherung),
percent of gross salary. Scope: Germany (§ 249 SGB V).

pflegeversicherung_pct.description:
Employer share of long-term care insurance (Pflegeversicherung),
percent of gross salary. Scope: Germany (§ 58 SGB XI).

rentenversicherung_pct.description:
Employer share of statutory pension insurance (Rentenversicherung),
percent of gross salary. Scope: Germany (§§ 157–169 SGB VI).

unfallversicherung_pct_estimate.description:
Estimated employer accident-insurance contribution (Unfallversicherung),
percent of gross salary. Scope: Germany (§ 150 SGB VII). Rate varies
by Berufsgenossenschaft risk class — estimate only.

EmploymentType.description:
Employment type enumerating the contractual relationship kinds observed
across UNSTARTER, ACME, and German-law companies modelled in CorpoSpec.

Includes the German-law specific values (`angestellte`, `geschaeftsfuehrer`,
`minijob-520`, `werkstudent`) alongside internationally common categories.

oneOf.description: Standard German dependent employee (Arbeitsvertrag, § 611a BGB).
Managing director of a GmbH (statutory organ under GmbHG).
Full-time salaried employee (generic).
Part-time salaried employee.
German Minijob (geringfügige Beschäftigung up to €520 / month).
German Werkstudent (tertiary-student employment status).
Independent contractor (Werkvertrag / Dienstvertrag).
Freelance worker (selbstständig, 1099-equivalent).
Internship (Praktikum).
Advisor or board observer (non-employee).
Fallback for contractual arrangements not covered above.

first_hire.description:
Target date for the first hire into this role. Prose is accepted to
carry gating context (e.g. "post pre-seed close") that a pure date
would erase.

target_count.description:
Target count of filled positions at plan horizon. Prose is accepted
to carry ranges (e.g. "2-3 FDFs in first 12 months").

IpBoundaries.description:
IP boundary definitions for a role.

Relevant when the incumbent performs work for multiple legal entities
(e.g. Forward-Deployed Founder operating a personal company in parallel).

own_company_work.description:
Prose describing what IP remains with the role incumbent's own
company.

separation.description:
Prose describing the separation mechanism (IP-separation clause,
non-compete exclusions, time-logging, etc.).

template.description:
PathRef to the contract template that encodes these boundaries
(e.g. `legal/templates/fdf-arbeitsvertrag`).

unstarter_work.description: Prose describing what IP is assigned to the employing entity.

PathRef.description:
Path-based cross-reference relative to .corpospec/ root.
Pattern: `^[a-z0-9_-]+(/[a-z0-9_.-]+)+$`

RoleCustom.description:
Typed extension fields for Role.

Union of all role-specific custom fields observed in `.corpospec/` YAML
data across UNSTARTER, KICKGEIST and ACME.

bdr_reference.description:
PathRef to the BDR that authorises this role (e.g.
`governance/decisions/0021-forward-deployed-founder`).

benefits.description: Non-cash benefits attached to the role.

compensation_midpoint_eur.description: Midpoint of the compensation band, in EUR.

compensation_schedule.description: Two-tier compensation schedule tied to a funding trigger.

description.description: Free-form description of the role's remit.

employer_contributions_pct_estimate.description:
Aggregate estimate of employer-side social-insurance contributions
as a percentage of gross salary.

employment_contract.description: PathRef to the legal template backing this contract.

employment_entity.description:
PathRef to the legal entity that employs this role (e.g.
`entity/unstarter-gmbh`).

employment_jurisdiction.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the employing jurisdiction.

hiring_after.description:
PathRef to another role that must be filled before this one (or to
a milestone under `governance/`). Observed values are role PathRefs.

hiring_priority.description: Integer priority ordering within the hiring plan (`1` = highest).

hiring_start_target.description:
Target start for hiring into this role.

Union type: may be a plain ISO date string, a date annotated with
trailing prose (e.g. `"2026-09-01 (post pre-seed close)"`), or a
structured object keying multiple slots (e.g.
`{ fdf_1: "2026-09-15", fdf_2: "2027-01-15" }`). A stricter type
would truncate real annotations or reject the multi-slot shape.

ip_boundaries.description: IP boundary rules for multi-entity work.

minimum_wage_compliance.description:
Prose note on compliance with the statutory minimum wage
(§ 1 MiLoG, Germany — currently €12.82/hour).

scaling_note.description: Prose note on how the role scales as the company grows.

sozialversicherung_status.description:
Statutory social-insurance status (see
`PersonCustom.sozialversicherung_status` for rationale on the
free-form type).

time_allocation.description:
Time-allocation plan (relevant where a role splits time across
multiple workstreams).

vesting_tie.description: Prose describing conditions tying vesting to continued service.

vsop_grant_range.description: Prose range of Virtual Stock Option Plan (VSOP) grants for this role.

vsop_template.description: PathRef to the VSOP contract template.

vsop_vesting.description: Prose describing the VSOP vesting schedule.

RoleCustomStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a `Role` record.

Distinct from `StatusActive` (which models entities such as companies or
products): here we track the hiring lifecycle of an individual seat.

oneOf.description: Role exists on the plan but recruiting has not started.
Role is actively recruiting.
Recruiting is temporarily paused.
Role is filled by at least one incumbent.
Role was cancelled before being filled.
Role was filled and later retired; kept for historical reference.
Executive / C-level (CEO, CTO, CFO, Geschäftsführer).
Vice President.
Director level — manages managers or a large function.

Lead — leads a small team or workstream without a formal management
title.
Individual contributor.
Partner — professional-services or law-firm partnership rank.
Associate — early-career professional-services or legal role.
Principal — senior IC or senior consultant.
Manager — first-line people manager.
Supervisor — shift or small-team supervisor (blue-collar/ops).
Foreman — site-level team lead (construction, trades).
Technician — skilled-trade IC.
Fellow — research or academic appointment.
Resident — trainee clinical or research appointment.

RoleRequirements.description: Role requirements (must-have / nice-to-have skills and traits).

must_have.description: Requirements the incumbent must satisfy to qualify for the role.

ScheinselbstaendigkeitSafeguards.description:
Scheinselbstaendigkeit risk safeguards.

Under German law (§ 7 SGB IV), an apparently self-employed contractor may
be reclassified as a dependent employee if integration with the employer
is too deep. This record documents the mitigation strategy.

legal_basis.description: Statutory basis cited (e.g. `§ 7 Abs. 4 SGB IV`).

required_mitigation.description:
Concrete mitigation clauses — free-form prose because real
mitigations combine legal requirements with operational commitments
(e.g. attestation cadence, revenue-mix thresholds).

risk_assessment.description: Prose describing the residual risk after mitigations apply.

TimeAllocation.description: Time allocation plan for a role.

TimeAllocationModel.description:
Model governing how a role's time is allocated between competing work
streams.

oneOf.description:
Split between UNSTARTER product work and the incumbent's own company
(Forward-Deployed Founder model).
Fixed hours per week (e.g. standard 40h full-time).
Allocation drifts with active projects.
Negotiated case-by-case between employer and role incumbent.
Any other model documented in prose.

TimeAllocationOption.description: Time allocation option for a role (e.g. FDF split model).

description.description: Free-form description of when this split is appropriate.

split.description:
Split expressed as `X/Y` where `X` is the UNSTARTER share and `Y`
is the own-company share (e.g. `70/30`).

description: Role definition aligned with W3C ORG Post and Role.

abbreviation.description:
Short uppercase code used in diagrams and cap-table shorthand
(e.g. `"CTO"`, `"GF"`, `"FDF"`). Optional — not every role has a
stable abbreviation.

custom.description: Typed custom extension fields (employment, benefits, hiring plan).

headcount.description: Approved headcount for this role. `0` means "approved but unfilled".

reports_to.description:
PathRef of the role this role reports to. `None` only for the
top-of-hierarchy role (e.g. Geschäftsführer, CEO).

responsibilities.description:
List of responsibilities owned by this role. Mirrors
`OrgUnit.planned_roles`: a unit's planned roles are realised here.

title.description:
Human-readable title as it would appear on a business card
(e.g. `"Chief Technology Officer"`).

unit.description:
Id of the `OrgUnit` this role lives in. Local identifier (not a
PathRef) because the unit set is defined inline on `OrgStructure`.

title: Role
